agreement: Pizza Hut, LLC Location Franchise Agreement

obligations: Franchise grant for specified System Restaurant Concepts at approved Locations
Initial Term: 10 years from opening date per restaurant
Two 5-year renewal terms available (Article 22 conditions)
Renewal agreement may materially differ from current FA in ALL respects
Renewal protections: no initial franchise fee, same Protected Radius, royalty not greater than similarly situated renewers
Monthly Service Fee: 6.0% of Gross Sales (6.5% certain circumstances)
System Advertising Fund: 4.75% of Gross Sales
Digital Innovation Fee: $0.41/digital transaction
Restaurant Technology Fee: $2,950/year (12% annual cap, higher with TechGov approval)
Initial Franchise Fee: $25,000
Renewal Fee: $12,500/restaurant (up to $25,000)
Transfer Fee: $2,500 + $250/restaurant + outside counsel fees
Dragontail KMX: $240 install + $75/month (if DaaS)
Interim Operation Fee: Greater of 2x salary or 10% monthly GS + expenses
Must pay PHLLC's attorneys/experts fees if losing party in litigation
Must secure PHLLC approval of proposed Location (final determination)
Must sign lease approved by PHLLC (with Appendix D Lease Rider attached)
Must open first restaurant within 6 months of FA effective date
Additional restaurants within 12 months of site approval
Must perform asset upgrades (Major Remodels, Minor Remodels, Rebuilds, Relocations) as directed
Minor/Major Remodel not required within 5 years of last remodel
Rebuild/Relocation not required within 10 years of last rebuild
PHLLC retains right to impose new Brand Standards requiring improvements at any time
Relocation requires written approval; new franchise fee if not opened within 12 months
Satellite locations require advance written approval
Time is of the essence on all Article 5 requirements
Must strictly comply with Brand Standards (modifiable without consent)
Brand Standards changes will not materially alter rights/obligations under FA
Must complete Initial Training Program (First Slice: 6-12 weeks)
BCM mandatory for all employees within 30 days of hire
Must attend mandatory conferences/conventions at own expense
Must maintain one or more Qualified Operators with ≥10% equity interest
QO must attend and pass Initial Training Program
PHLLC may revoke QO approval based on performance review
Must open within 30 days of completing construction
Must give 10 days prior written notice of opening
Must use PHLLC-designated Computer System (POS, BOH, KM, HutBot)
Must sign SUS/FMS License Agreement, QuikOrder Agreement, HutBot Agreement
Must use Dragontail KMX if offering DaaS delivery
Must purchase proprietary products only from PHLLC/RSCS/Approved Distributors
Must join Pizza Hut Purchasing Co-op ($10 + $400/restaurant)
Must purchase virtually all goods through RSCS/Co-op programs
Must obtain insurance meeting Brand Standards minimums
Must sign Pepsi-Cola Adoption Agreement (exclusive through Dec 31, 2026)
Must accurately record all information through Computer System
Must grant PHLLC access to all Computer System components — no contractual limitation
No exclusive territory
Protected Radius: 500 yards — same concept type only
Delivery Area: contingent on Adequate Delivery Service per Brand Standards
PHLLC reserves broad rights within Protected Radius (other concepts, alternative channels, non-traditional locations, acquisitions)
Must provide Adequate Delivery Service or face delivery area reduction
Failure to cure inadequate delivery may result in termination
No right to participate in PHLLC's other business activities
Must waive and release all claims related to PHLLC competitive activities
PHLLC may terminate only for cause
30-day cure for most curable defaults (10 days for repeated payment; 24 hours for trademark)
Non-curable: unauthorized closure >15 days, felony, unauthorized transfer, fraud, bankruptcy, cross-default, cease operating
Cross-default: breach of other agreements with PHLLC or affiliates triggers termination right
Force Majeure >90 days: may terminate on 30 days notice
triggers: Failure to comply with FA or Brand Standards
Failure to pay amounts owed (10-day cure second default in 12 months)
Failure to provide Adequate Delivery Service
Conviction of felony / fraud / moral turpitude

obligations: Any transfer requires prior written consent with 30 days advance notice
Permitted transfers: existing owner, employee benefit, wholly-owned entity
Transferee must meet qualifications, complete training, sign then-current FA
PHLLC right of first refusal to match any offer
PHLLC must determine transfer price won't jeopardize economic viability
You must sign termination agreement, general release
Transfer fee: $2,500 + $250/restaurant + outside counsel fees
During term: cannot be involved in pizza/pasta/similar food business or Competitor
Competitor: derives >10% revenue from similar products
Cannot sell/lease premises to Competitor during term
Post-term 18 months: no similar food business within 25-mile radius of any PH restaurant, any county with PH, or 10 miles of any U.S. System Restaurant
Post-term 2 years: cannot sell/lease premises to Competitor
Applies to you and all Related Persons
Mediation first via CPR National Franchise Mediation Program
Mediation venue: Plano, TX (where PHLLC principal place of business)
Mediation excludes: trademark, confidential info, money owed, system-wide issues
Litigation venue: Collin County, TX (state, county, judicial district of principal place of business)
PHLLC may bring certain actions in any court with jurisdiction
Texas governing law
Loser pays attorneys' fees, experts' fees, court costs
Each owner with ≥10% interest must sign personal guaranty
Guaranty is unconditional and continuing
Guarantor liable for all obligations of Franchisee under FA
Guaranty survives amendment, compromise, release, or alteration of FA
Release, substitution, or addition of guarantor does not release other guarantors
Broad indemnification of PHLLC from losses arising from FA execution, operations, employment practices, third-party claims
If PHLLC becomes party to any proceeding from your acts/omissions, you must reimburse attorneys, experts, court costs, travel, lodging
Pay all money owed including termination damages (lost profits, lost opportunities, all costs)
Stop using Pizza Hut Marks immediately
Cancel assumed name registrations within 5 days
Honor PHLLC right to operate restaurants during post-termination period
Deliver all confidential info, training materials, Brand Standards, software, customer lists
Transfer phone numbers to PHLLC
De-identify restaurant (or PHLLC may do at your cost)
Sell secret spices/trade secret items to PHLLC at cost
Surrender all computer software and electronic media
Force Majeure excuses performance for duration of event
If Force Majeure extends >90 days, PHLLC may terminate on 30 days notice
